content: Any autonomous agent, whether biological, chemical, or artificial, can be framed as a dynamic, self-pruning Tree of Thoughts where each branch corresponds to a concrete effect the agent exerts on its environment, rather than just an abstract cognitive step. This framing redefines agentic power not as a single ability to act, but as the agent’s capacity to selectively expand or prune its action branches to achieve sustained effects on its surroundings.
